mnemonics: Thread Libraries: Pthreads are POSIX Threads, think 'P' for Portable across UNIX systems. Windows uses its own API, remember 'W' for Windows.
keyPoints: Thread libraries provide an API for creating and managing threads.
Pthreads is a standard for UNIX-like systems, while Windows has its own thread API.
Using thread libraries allows for writing multi-threaded programs that can run on different operating systems with appropriate libraries.
commonMistakes: Confusing Pthreads with Windows threads; they are different APIs.
Assuming thread library functions are the same across all operating systems; they are not standardized.
Forgetting to link against the required thread library (e.g., -lpthread for Pthreads).
